Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»EXACT time South Africa’s 100m sprinters will run in the semi-finals

It’s a busy day for Team South Africa at the Paris Olympics 2024 on Sunday, 4 August with all eyes on the men’s 100m sprinters.

It’s a busy day forTeam South Africaat theParis Olympics 2024onSunday, 4 Augustwith all eyes on the men’s 100m sprinters.

Enjoy the Paris Olympic Games 2024 experience at your favourite ATKV Resort!

Team SA hasthreesprinters qualified for the men’s 100m semi-finals and will be looking ahead to one of them, at least, making the final.

Mzansi’s three sprinters have all been drawn in different semi-finals.

Only the top two finishers in each semi-final – plus the next two fastest times – will progress to the eight-man final.

Shaun Maswanganyifaces a daunting task in the first semi-final at20:05(SA time) when he lines up alongside gold medal favourite Noah Lyles from the United States, Jamaica’s Oblique Seville and Great Britain’s Louie Hinchliffe, among others.

Akani Simbinewill run in the second semi-final at20:14(SA time) with Jamaica’s Ackeem Blake, defending Olympic 100m champion Lamont Jacobs (Italy), Botswana’s Letsile Tebogo and American Kenny Bednarek also among the favourites.

Benjamin Richardson, who only qualified for the semi-finals as one of the fastest non-automatic qualifiers from the heats, will be hard-pressed to reach the final. He lines up alongside Great Britain’s Zharnel Hughes, American Fred Kerley, Kenya’s Ferdinand Omanyala and Jamaica’s Kishane Thompson – among others – at20:23(SA time).

Team SA started the day withfourmedals with Tatjana Smith (gold and silver) and the men’s sevens rugby and Alan Hatherly (bronze) putting them in a tie for27thplace on the overall table.

Men’s long jump qualification, Group A (11:00):Jovan van Vuuren

Men’s long jump qualification, Group B (11:00):Cheswill Johnson

Women’s 400m H, first round, heats (12:43):Rogail Joseph

Women’s 400m H, first round, heats (12:51):Zeney Gledenhuys

Men’s 400m, round 1 heats (19:21):Lythe Pillay

Men’s 400m, round 1 heats (19:37):Zakhiti Nene

Men’s 100m, semi-finals (20:05):Shaun Maswanganyi

Men’s 100m, semi-finals (20:14):Akani Simbine

Men’s 100m, semi-finals (20:23):Benjamin Richardson

Women’s 800m, semi-finals (20:55):Prudence Sekgodiso

Women’s road race (14:00):Tiffany Keep, Ashleigh Moolman Pasio

Men’s Individual, fourth round

Erik van Rooyen – 11:44

Christiaan Bezuidenhout – 11:55
